---
title: "flat_partytrees.Rmd empty"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r development, include=FALSE}
library(testthat)
```

<!--
 You need to run the 'description' chunk in the '0-dev_history.Rmd' file before continuing your code there.
-->



```{r development-load}
# Load already included functions if relevant
pkgload::load_all(export_all = FALSE)
```



## SHAP helpers

```{r development-shap_helpers}
# Helper functions used by the SHAP decomposition workflow
```

```{r function-assert_data_table_available}
#' Assert that the data.table package is available
#' @return Invisibly returns `NULL` if the check passes. Otherwise, throws an
#'   error.
#' @details This function is used internally to ensure that the `data.table`
#'   package is available before attempting to use it. If `data.table` is not
#'   installed, an informative error message is thrown instructing the user to
#'   install the package.
#' @export
.assert_data_table_available <- function() {
  if (!requireNamespace("data.table", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop(
      paste(
        "Package `data.table` is required.",
        "Install it with install.packages(\"data.table\")."
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }
}
```

```{r function-assert_scalar_flag}
#' Assert that an input is a single logical value
#' @param x The input to check.
#' @param arg The name of the argument being checked, used in error messages.
#' @return Invisibly returns `NULL` if the check passes. Otherwise, throws an
#'   error.
#' @details This function is used internally to validate that certain inputs
#'   are single logical values (flags). It checks that `x` is of type
#'   logical, has length 1, and is not NA. If any of these conditions fail,
#'   an informative error message is thrown indicating the argument name and
#'   the nature of the issue.
#' @export
.assert_scalar_flag <- function(x, arg) {
  if (!is.logical(x) || length(x) != 1L || is.na(x)) {
    stop(sprintf("`%s` must be TRUE or FALSE.", arg), call. = FALSE)
  }
}
```

```{r function-assert_scalar_numeric}
#' Assert that an input is a single finite numeric value
#' @param x The input to check.
#' @param arg The name of the argument being checked, used in error messages.
#' @return Invisibly returns `NULL` if the check passes. Otherwise, throws an
#'   error.
#' @details This function is used internally to validate that certain inputs
#'   are single numeric values that are not NA or infinite. It checks that
#'   `x` is numeric, has length 1, and is not NA or infinite. If any of these
#'   conditions fail, an informative error message is thrown indicating the
#'   argument name and the nature of the issue.
#' @export
.assert_scalar_numeric <- function(x, arg) {
  if (!is.numeric(x) || length(x) != 1L || is.na(x) || !is.finite(x)) {
    stop(
      sprintf("`%s` must be a single finite numeric value.", arg),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }
}
```

```{r function-validate_shap_table}
#' Validate a SHAP table
#' Ensure that the input SHAP table is a data.table with at least one row and
#' one numeric column. Optionally check for missing values and trigger an error
#' any are found.
#' @param shap_dt A data.table representing SHAP values to validate.
#' @param allow_missing Logical scalar. If `TRUE`, allow missing values in
#'   SHAP columns. If `FALSE`, trigger an error if any SHAP column contains
#'   missing values.
#' @return The input `shap_dt` if it passes validation checks.
#' @details This function is used internally to ensure that SHAP tables are in
#'   a consistent format with appropriate types before decomposition. It checks
#'   that the input is a data.table (or coercible to one), contains at least
#'   one row and one column, that all columns are numeric, and optionally that
#'   there are no missing values.
#' @export
.validate_shap_table <- function(shap_dt, allow_missing = FALSE) {
  if (!data.table::is.data.table(shap_dt)) {
    shap_dt <- tryCatch(
      data.table::as.data.table(shap_dt),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (is.null(shap_dt) || !data.table::is.data.table(shap_dt)) {
      stop("`shap_dt` must be a data.table or coercible to one.", call. = FALSE)
    }
  }

  if (nrow(shap_dt) == 0L) {
    stop("`shap_dt` must contain at least one row.", call. = FALSE)
  }
  if (ncol(shap_dt) == 0L) {
    stop(
      "`shap_dt` must contain at least one SHAP feature column.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  is_numeric_col <- vapply(shap_dt, is.numeric, logical(1L))
  if (!all(is_numeric_col)) {
    bad_cols <- names(shap_dt)[!is_numeric_col]
    stop(
      sprintf(
        "All SHAP columns must be numeric. Non-numeric columns: %s",
        paste(bad_cols, collapse = ", ")
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  has_missing <- vapply(shap_dt, anyNA, logical(1L))
  if (!allow_missing && any(has_missing)) {
    bad_cols <- names(shap_dt)[has_missing]
    stop(
      sprintf(
        "SHAP columns must not contain missing values. Columns with NA: %s",
        paste(bad_cols, collapse = ", ")
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  shap_dt
}
```

```{r function-prepare_named_shap_dt}
#' Prepare a SHAP table with named numeric columns
#' Ensure that the input SHAP table is a data.table with named numeric columns.
#' If the input is coercible to a data.table but has no column names,
#' assign default names
#' like "feature_1", "feature_2", etc. Validate that all columns are numeric and
#' optionally check for missing values.
#' @param shap A SHAP table to prepare.
#' @param allow_missing Logical scalar. If `TRUE`, allow missing values in
#'   SHAP columns. If `FALSE`, trigger an error if any SHAP column contains
#'   missing values.
#' @return A data.table with named numeric columns representing SHAP features.
#' @details This function is used internally to standardize SHAP tables
#'   before decomposition. It ensures that the SHAP data is in a consistent
#'   format with appropriate column names and types.
#' @export
.prepare_named_shap_dt <- function(shap, allow_missing = FALSE) {
  shap_dt <- tryCatch(
    data.table::copy(data.table::as.data.table(shap)),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if (is.null(shap_dt)) {
    stop("`shap` must be coercible to a rectangular table.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  if (is.null(names(shap_dt))) {
    data.table::setnames(shap_dt, paste0("feature_", seq_len(ncol(shap_dt))))
  } else {
    data.table::setnames(
      shap_dt,
      make.unique(ifelse(names(shap_dt) == "", "feature", names(shap_dt)))
    )
  }

  .validate_shap_table(shap_dt, allow_missing = allow_missing)
}
```

```{r function-dalex_shap_to_wide}
#' Reshape a DALEX-like long SHAP table to wide format
#'
#' Convert a long-format SHAP table with observation IDs, feature names, and
#' SHAP values to a wide format with one row per observation and one column per
#' feature. Baseline rows can be optionally dropped based on common baseline
#' labels.
#'
#' @param shap A long-format SHAP table containing observation IDs, feature
#'   names, and SHAP values.
#' @param id_col Optional column name identifying observations. If `NULL`,
#'   common ID column names will be searched for.
#' @param variable_col Optional column name containing feature names. If
#'   `NULL`, common variable column names will be searched for.
#' @param value_col Optional column name containing SHAP values. If
#'   `NULL`, common value column names will be searched for.
#' @param drop_baseline Logical scalar. If `TRUE`, rows with feature names
#'   matching common baseline labels will be dropped before reshaping.
#' @param baseline_labels Character vector of feature names to treat as
#'   baseline rows when `drop_baseline = TRUE`. Matching is case-sensitive.
#' @return A wide-format `data.table` with one row per observation and one
#'   numeric column per SHAP feature.
#' @details The function attempts to identify the ID, variable, and value
#'   columns based on common naming conventions. If `drop_baseline = TRUE`,
#'   rows with feature names in `baseline_labels` are removed before
#'   reshaping. The resulting wide table contains only numeric SHAP feature
#'   columns. Aliases for the identified columns are as follows:
#' - ID column candidates: `"_id_"`, `"id"`, `"row_id"`,
#'   `"observation_id"`, `"observation"`, `"case_id"`
#' - Variable column candidates: `"_vname_"`, `"variable"`,
#'   `"variable_name"`, `"feature"`
#' - Value column candidates: `"_attribution_"`, `"contribution"`,
#'   `"phi"`, `"shap"`, `"value"`
#' @export
.dalex_shap_to_wide <- function(
    shap,
    id_col = NULL,
    variable_col = NULL,
    value_col = NULL,
    drop_baseline = TRUE,
    baseline_labels = c("_baseline_", "baseline", "intercept")) {
  shap_dt <- data.table::copy(data.table::as.data.table(shap))

  if (nrow(shap_dt) == 0L) {
    stop("`shap` must contain at least one row.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  nms <- names(shap_dt)

  if (is.null(id_col)) {
    id_candidates <- c(
      "_id_", "id", "row_id", "observation_id", "observation", "case_id"
    )
    id_col <- intersect(id_candidates, nms)[1]
  }
  if (is.null(variable_col)) {
    variable_candidates <- c("_vname_", "variable", "variable_name", "feature")
    variable_col <- intersect(variable_candidates, nms)[1]
  }
  if (is.null(value_col)) {
    value_candidates <- c(
      "_attribution_", "contribution", "phi", "shap", "value"
    )
    value_col <- intersect(value_candidates, nms)[1]
  }

  if (is.na(variable_col) || is.null(variable_col)) {
    stop(
      paste(
        "Could not identify the DALEX feature-name column.",
        "Supply `variable_col`."
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }
  if (is.na(value_col) || is.null(value_col)) {
    stop(
      "Could not identify the DALEX SHAP-value column. Supply `value_col`.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  if (is.na(id_col) || is.null(id_col)) {
    shap_dt[[".observation_id__"]] <- 1L
    id_col <- ".observation_id__"
  }

  shap_dt[[variable_col]] <- as.character(shap_dt[[variable_col]])
  shap_dt[[value_col]] <- as.numeric(shap_dt[[value_col]])

  if (isTRUE(drop_baseline)) {
    shap_dt <- shap_dt[!(shap_dt[[variable_col]] %in% baseline_labels), ]
  }

  if (nrow(shap_dt) == 0L) {
    stop(
      "No feature-level SHAP rows remain after dropping baseline rows.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  quote_name <- function(x) {
    paste0("`", gsub("`", "\\\\`", x), "`")
  }

  wide_dt <- data.table::dcast(
    shap_dt,
    stats::as.formula(paste(quote_name(id_col), "~", quote_name(variable_col))),
    value.var = value_col
  )

  data.table::setorderv(wide_dt, id_col)
  wide_dt[[id_col]] <- NULL
  wide_dt
}
```


# shap_conc_decomp

```{r development-shap_conc_decomp}
# You can prepare the code of the shap_conc_decomp() function here
```

```{r function-shap_conc_decomp}
#' Decompose a concentration index using SHAP values
#'
#' Compute SHAP-based feature contributions to the concentration index of a
#' predicted outcome. The SHAP table can be supplied either in wide format
#' (one row per observation, one column per feature) or in DALEX-like long
#' format and reshaped internally.
#'
#' @param shap A rectangular SHAP table. By default this should be a wide table
#'   with one numeric feature column per SHAP contributor. If `from_dalex =
#'   TRUE`, `shap` should instead be a long DALEX-like table containing
#'   observation IDs, feature names, and SHAP values.
#' @param rank A numeric vector giving the socioeconomic ranking variable for
#'   each observation. When `type = "L"`, this is interpreted as the observed
#'   socioeconomic level variable.
#' @param type One of `"CI"`, `"CIg"`, `"CIc"`, or `"L"` selecting the
#'   decomposition target. The first three are rank-dependent concentration
#'   indices scored consistently with [ci_factory()]. `"L"` uses the
#'   Erreygers--Kessels level-dependent bivariate index based on observed
#'   socioeconomic levels.
#' @param prediction Optional numeric vector of model predictions. If omitted,
#'   predictions are reconstructed as `baseline + rowSums(shap)`.
#' @param baseline Optional scalar baseline prediction. Required when
#'   `prediction` is not supplied.
#' @param weights Optional numeric vector of non-negative case weights. If
#'   omitted, equal weights are used. Observations with non-positive weights do
#'   not contribute to the decomposition.
#' @param from_dalex Logical scalar. If `TRUE`, reshape a DALEX-like long SHAP
#'   table to wide format before decomposition.
#' @param dalex_id_col Optional column name identifying observations in the
#'   DALEX-like input.
#' @param dalex_variable_col Optional column name containing feature names in
#'   the DALEX-like input.
#' @param dalex_value_col Optional column name containing SHAP values in the
#'   DALEX-like input.
#' @param na_rm Logical scalar. If `TRUE`, drop rows with missing values in
#'   required inputs before computing the decomposition.
#' @param sort Logical scalar. If `TRUE`, order output features by decreasing
#'   absolute SHAP contribution.
#' @param tolerance Numeric tolerance used to detect numerically zero mean
#'   predictions or concentration indices.
#'
#' @return A list of class `"shap_ci_decomposition"` with two components:
#'   `diagnostics`, a one-row `data.table` of decomposition diagnostics, and
#'   `contributions`, a `data.table` containing one row per SHAP feature with
#'   columns `feature`, `D_k_SHAP`, `pct_contribution`, and
#'   `abs_contribution`.
#'
#' @details The total `concentration_index` reported in `diagnostics` is the
#'   non-negative score returned by `ci_factory(type)`. Feature contributions
#'   are computed on the signed linear scale for the selected index and then
#'   oriented so they sum to the non-negative factory score when the SHAP values
#'   reconstruct the prediction.
#'
#'   For rank-dependent types, weighted fractional ranks and the same unbiased
#'   weighted covariance convention used by [ci_factory()] are used. `"CI"`
#'   divides feature covariances by the weighted mean prediction, `"CIg"` uses
#'   the generalized covariance form, and `"CIc"` applies the prediction-range
#'   correction.
#'
#'   For `type = "L"`, `rank` is treated as an observed socioeconomic level
#'   `s_i`. The decomposition uses the weighted mean of
#'   `((s_i - mu_s) / mu_s) * phi_ik` for each feature, where `mu_s` is the
#'   weighted mean socioeconomic level.
#'
#' @export
shap_conc_decomp <- function(
    shap,
    rank,
    type = c("CI", "CIg", "CIc", "L"),
    prediction = NULL,
    baseline = NULL,
    weights = NULL,
    from_dalex = FALSE,
    dalex_id_col = NULL,
    dalex_variable_col = NULL,
    dalex_value_col = NULL,
    na_rm = FALSE,
    sort = TRUE,
    tolerance = sqrt(.Machine$double.eps)) {
  .assert_data_table_available()
  .assert_scalar_flag(from_dalex, "from_dalex")
  .assert_scalar_flag(na_rm, "na_rm")
  .assert_scalar_flag(sort, "sort")
  .assert_scalar_numeric(tolerance, "tolerance")
  type <- match.arg(type)

  if (isTRUE(from_dalex)) {
    shap <- .dalex_shap_to_wide(
      shap = shap,
      id_col = dalex_id_col,
      variable_col = dalex_variable_col,
      value_col = dalex_value_col
    )
  }

  shap_dt <- .prepare_named_shap_dt(shap, allow_missing = na_rm)
  n <- nrow(shap_dt)

  if (length(rank) != n) {
    stop("`rank` must have length equal to nrow(`shap`).", call. = FALSE)
  }
  if (!is.null(prediction) && length(prediction) != n) {
    stop("`prediction` must have length equal to nrow(`shap`).", call. = FALSE)
  }
  if (is.null(weights)) {
    weights <- rep.int(1, n)
  } else if (!is.numeric(weights) || length(weights) != n) {
    stop("`weights` must be numeric with length equal to nrow(`shap`).",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  } else if (any(!is.finite(weights) & !is.na(weights))) {
    stop("`weights` must contain only finite values or NA.", call. = FALSE)
  } else if (anyNA(weights) && !na_rm) {
    stop(
      "`weights` contains missing values. Set `na_rm = TRUE` to drop ",
      "incomplete rows.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }
  if (is.null(prediction)) {
    if (is.null(baseline)) {
      stop(
        "Supply either `prediction` or `baseline` so predictions can",
        " be identified.",
        call. = FALSE
      )
    }
    .assert_scalar_numeric(baseline, "baseline")
  } else if (!is.numeric(prediction)) {
    stop("`prediction` must be numeric.", call. = FALSE)
  } else if (anyNA(prediction) && !na_rm) {
    stop(
      "`prediction` contains missing values. Set `na_rm = TRUE` to drop ",
      "incomplete rows.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  if (anyNA(rank) && !na_rm) {
    stop(
      "`rank` contains missing values. Set `na_rm = TRUE` to drop ",
      "incomplete rows.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  working_dt <- data.table::copy(shap_dt)
  working_dt[["rank__"]] <- rank
  working_dt[["weights__"]] <- as.numeric(weights)

  if (!is.null(prediction)) {
    working_dt[["prediction__"]] <- as.numeric(prediction)
  }

  if (na_rm) {
    required_cols <- c(
      names(shap_dt),
      "rank__",
      "weights__",
      if (!is.null(prediction)) "prediction__"
    )
    keep <- stats::complete.cases(working_dt[, required_cols, with = FALSE])
    working_dt <- working_dt[keep, ]
  }

  if (nrow(working_dt) == 0L) {
    stop("No complete cases remain after applying `na_rm`.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  working_dt <- working_dt[working_dt[["weights__"]] > 0, ]

  if (nrow(working_dt) == 0L) {
    stop("No positive-weight complete cases remain.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  if (is.null(prediction)) {
    working_dt[["prediction__"]] <- rowSums(
      working_dt[, names(shap_dt), with = FALSE]
    ) + baseline
    prediction_source <- "baseline + rowSums(shap)"
  } else {
    prediction_source <- "prediction"
  }

  n_complete <- nrow(working_dt)
  rank_vector <- working_dt[["rank__"]]
  prediction_vector <- working_dt[["prediction__"]]
  weight_vector <- working_dt[["weights__"]]
  weight_sum <- sum(weight_vector)
  weight_prob <- weight_vector / weight_sum
  mu_hat <- stats::weighted.mean(prediction_vector, weight_vector)

  if (type %in% c("CI", "CIg", "CIc")) {
    rank_fraction <- rank_wt(rank_vector, weight_vector)
    mean_rank <- sum(weight_prob * rank_fraction)
    index_weight <- rank_fraction - mean_rank
    cov_denom <- 1 - sum(weight_prob^2)

    weighted_cov_with_rank <- function(values) {
      if (!is.finite(cov_denom) || cov_denom <= tolerance) {
        return(0)
      }
      mean_value <- sum(weight_prob * values)
      sum(weight_prob * index_weight * (values - mean_value)) / cov_denom
    }

    if (type == "CI" && abs(mu_hat) <= tolerance) {
      stop(
        paste(
          "The mean prediction is numerically zero, so the",
          "concentration index is undefined."
        ),
        call. = FALSE
      )
    }

    prediction_range <- diff(range(prediction_vector))
    if (type == "CIc" &&
        (!is.finite(prediction_range) || prediction_range <= tolerance)) {
      stop(
        paste(
          "The prediction range is numerically zero, so the",
          "corrected concentration index is undefined."
        ),
        call. = FALSE
      )
    }

    scale_factor <- switch(type,
      CI = 2 / mu_hat,
      CIg = 2,
      CIc = 8 / prediction_range
    )
    contribution_score <- function(values) {
      scale_factor * weighted_cov_with_rank(values)
    }
    signed_total_ci <- contribution_score(prediction_vector)
  } else {
    mu_s <- stats::weighted.mean(rank_vector, weight_vector)
    if (!is.finite(mu_s) || abs(mu_s) <= tolerance) {
      stop(
        paste(
          "The mean socioeconomic level is numerically zero, so the",
          "level-dependent index is undefined."
        ),
        call. = FALSE
      )
    }

    index_weight <- (rank_vector - mu_s) / mu_s
    contribution_score <- function(values) {
      sum(weight_prob * index_weight * values)
    }
    signed_total_ci <- contribution_score(prediction_vector)
  }

  ci_fun <- ci_factory(type)
  ci_score <- ci_fun(cbind(rank = rank_vector, outcome = prediction_vector),
    weight_vector
  )

  score_direction <- if (signed_total_ci < 0) -1 else 1
  contribution_wide <- data.table::as.data.table(
    as.list(vapply(
      names(shap_dt),
      function(col) score_direction * contribution_score(working_dt[[col]]),
      numeric(1L)
    ))
  )

  contributions <- data.table::melt(
    contribution_wide,
    measure.vars = names(contribution_wide),
    variable.name = "feature",
    value.name = "D_k_SHAP",
    variable.factor = FALSE
  )

  shap_total <- sum(contributions[["D_k_SHAP"]])

  contributions[["pct_contribution"]] <- if (abs(ci_score) <= tolerance) {
    rep(NA_real_, nrow(contributions))
  } else {
    100 * contributions[["D_k_SHAP"]] / ci_score
  }
  contributions[["abs_contribution"]] <- abs(contributions[["D_k_SHAP"]])

  if (sort) {
    data.table::setorderv(
      contributions,
      cols = c("abs_contribution", "feature"),
      order = c(-1L, 1L)
    )
  }

  diagnostics <- data.table::data.table(
    n = n_complete,
    weight_sum = weight_sum,
    type = type,
    mean_prediction = mu_hat,
    concentration_index = ci_score,
    signed_concentration_index = signed_total_ci,
    score_direction = score_direction,
    shap_sum = shap_total,
    additivity_gap = ci_score - shap_total,
    centered_rank_sum = sum(weight_prob * index_weight),
    prediction_source = prediction_source
  )

  structure(
    list(
      diagnostics = diagnostics,
      contributions = contributions[]
    ),
    class = "shap_ci_decomposition"
  )
}
```

```{r example-shap_conc_decomp}
shap <- data.frame(
  education = c(1, 0, -1),
  water = c(0, 1, 1)
)
rank <- c(1, 2, 3)

res <- shap_conc_decomp(
  shap = shap,
  rank = rank,
  baseline = 2
)
res$diagnostics
res$contributions

dalex_like <- data.frame(
  "_id_" = rep(1:3, each = 3),
  "_vname_" = rep(c("_baseline_", "education", "water"), times = 3),
  "_attribution_" = c(2, 1, 0, 2, 0, 1, 2, -1, 1),
  check.names = FALSE
)

shap_conc_decomp(
  shap = dalex_like,
  rank = rank,
  baseline = 2,
  from_dalex = TRUE
)$contributions
```


# fractional_rank

```{r development-fractional_rank}
# You can prepare the code of the fractional_rank() function here
```

```{r function-fractional_rank}
#' Compute fractional ranks
#'
#' Convert a numeric ranking variable to fractional ranks in `(0, 1)` using
#' average ranks for ties. This is the standard rank definition used in
#' concentration-index calculations.
#'
#' @param x An atomic vector to rank.
#' @param ties_method Tie-handling method passed to `data.table::frankv()`.
#'   Only `"average"` is supported.
#' @param na_rm Logical scalar. If `TRUE`, missing values are dropped before
#'   ranking. If `FALSE`, missing values trigger an error.
#'
#' @return A numeric vector of fractional ranks with length equal to the number
#'   of retained observations.
#'
#' @export
fractional_rank <- function(
    x,
    ties_method = "average",
    na_rm = FALSE) {
  .assert_data_table_available()
  .assert_scalar_flag(na_rm, "na_rm")

  if (!identical(ties_method, "average")) {
    stop(
      paste(
        "`ties_method` must be \"average\" to preserve the standard",
        "fractional-rank definition."
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }
  if (!is.atomic(x)) {
    stop("`x` must be an atomic vector.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  if (na_rm) {
    x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  } else if (anyNA(x)) {
    stop(
      paste(
        "`x` contains missing values.",
        "Set `na_rm = TRUE` to drop them."
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  n <- length(x)
  if (n == 0L) {
    stop("`x` must contain at least one non-missing value.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  (data.table::frankv(x, ties.method = ties_method) - 0.5) / n
}
```

```{r example-fractional_rank}
fractional_rank(c(10, 20, 20, 40))
fractional_rank(c(1, NA, 3), na_rm = TRUE)
```

```{r tests-fractional_rank}
test_that("fractional_rank computes average fractional ranks", {
  expect_equal(
    fractional_rank(c(10, 20, 20, 40)),
    c(0.125, 0.5, 0.5, 0.875)
  )
})

test_that("fractional_rank handles missing values and validates inputs", {
  expect_equal(
    fractional_rank(c(1, NA, 3), na_rm = TRUE),
    c(0.25, 0.75)
  )

  expect_error(fractional_rank(c(1, NA, 3)), "missing values")
  expect_error(fractional_rank(c(1, 2), ties_method = "first"), "ties_method")
  expect_error(fractional_rank(list(1, 2)), "atomic vector")
})
```

```{r tests-shap_conc_decomp}
test_that("shap_conc_decomp decomposes a wide SHAP table", {
  shap <- data.frame(
    education = c(1, 0, -1),
    water = c(0, 1, 1)
  )

  out <- shap_conc_decomp(
    shap = shap,
    rank = c(1, 2, 3),
    baseline = 2
  )

  expect_s3_class(out, "shap_ci_decomposition")
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$n, 3)
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$weight_sum, 3)
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$mean_prediction, 8 / 3)
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$concentration_index, 1 / 8)
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$signed_concentration_index, -1 / 8)
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$score_direction, -1)
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$shap_sum, 1 / 8)
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$additivity_gap, 0, tolerance = 1e-12)
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$centered_rank_sum, 0, tolerance = 1e-12)
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$prediction_source, "baseline + rowSums(shap)")

  expect_equal(out$contributions$feature, c("education", "water"))
  expect_equal(out$contributions$D_k_SHAP, c(1 / 4, -1 / 8))
  expect_equal(out$contributions$pct_contribution, c(200, -100))
  expect_equal(out$contributions$abs_contribution, c(1 / 4, 1 / 8))
})

test_that("shap_conc_decomp reshapes DALEX-like SHAP tables", {
  wide_shap <- data.frame(
    education = c(1, 0, -1),
    water = c(0, 1, 1)
  )
  dalex_like <- data.frame(
    "_id_" = rep(1:3, each = 3),
    "_vname_" = rep(c("_baseline_", "education", "water"), times = 3),
    "_attribution_" = c(2, 1, 0, 2, 0, 1, 2, -1, 1),
    check.names = FALSE
  )

  from_wide <- shap_conc_decomp(
    shap = wide_shap,
    rank = c(1, 2, 3),
    baseline = 2
  )
  from_dalex <- shap_conc_decomp(
    shap = dalex_like,
    rank = c(1, 2, 3),
    baseline = 2,
    from_dalex = TRUE
  )

  expect_equal(from_dalex$diagnostics, from_wide$diagnostics)
  expect_equal(
    from_dalex$contributions[, c("feature", "D_k_SHAP")],
    from_wide$contributions[, c("feature", "D_k_SHAP")]
  )
})

test_that("shap_conc_decomp drops incomplete rows with na_rm", {
  shap <- data.frame(
    x1 = c(1, NA, 0, 2),
    x2 = c(0, 1, 1, 1)
  )
  prediction <- c(2, 3, NA, 4)

  out <- shap_conc_decomp(
    shap = shap,
    rank = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
    prediction = prediction,
    na_rm = TRUE,
    sort = FALSE
  )

  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$n, 2)
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$prediction_source, "prediction")
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$concentration_index, 1 / 3)
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$additivity_gap, 0, tolerance = 1e-12)
  expect_equal(out$contributions$feature, c("x1", "x2"))
  expect_equal(out$contributions$D_k_SHAP, c(1 / 6, 1 / 6))
})

test_that("shap_conc_decomp uses ci_factory scores for weighted variants", {
  shap <- data.frame(
    education = c(1, 0, -1, 1),
    water = c(0, 1, 1, -1)
  )
  rank <- c(4, 1, 3, 2)
  weights <- c(1, 2, 1, 3)
  prediction <- 2 + rowSums(shap)

  for (type in c("CI", "CIg", "CIc")) {
    out <- shap_conc_decomp(
      shap = shap,
      rank = rank,
      type = type,
      baseline = 2,
      weights = weights,
      sort = FALSE
    )

    expect_equal(
      out$diagnostics$concentration_index,
      ci_factory(type)(cbind(rank = rank, outcome = prediction), weights)
    )
    expect_equal(
      out$diagnostics$shap_sum,
      out$diagnostics$concentration_index,
      tolerance = 1e-12
    )
    expect_equal(out$diagnostics$weight_sum, sum(weights))
  }
})

test_that("shap_conc_decomp supports level-dependent L decomposition", {
  shap <- data.frame(
    education = c(1, 0, -1),
    water = c(0, 1, 1)
  )

  out <- shap_conc_decomp(
    shap = shap,
    rank = c(1, 2, 3),
    type = "L",
    baseline = 2,
    sort = FALSE
  )

  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$type, "L")
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$concentration_index, 1 / 6)
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$signed_concentration_index, -1 / 6)
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$shap_sum, 1 / 6)
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$additivity_gap, 0, tolerance = 1e-12)
  expect_equal(out$diagnostics$centered_rank_sum, 0, tolerance = 1e-12)

  expect_equal(out$contributions$feature, c("education", "water"))
  expect_equal(out$contributions$D_k_SHAP, c(1 / 3, -1 / 6))
  expect_equal(out$contributions$pct_contribution, c(200, -100))
})

test_that("shap_conc_decomp validates key inputs", {
  shap <- data.frame(x1 = c(1, -1), x2 = c(0, 0))

  expect_error(
    shap_conc_decomp(shap = shap, rank = 1:3, baseline = 0),
    "length equal"
  )
  expect_error(
    shap_conc_decomp(shap = shap, rank = c(1, 2)),
    "Supply either `prediction` or `baseline`"
  )
  expect_error(
    shap_conc_decomp(shap = shap, rank = c(1, 2), baseline = 0),
    "mean prediction is numerically zero"
  )
  expect_error(
    shap_conc_decomp(
      shap = data.frame(x1 = c(1, NA)),
      rank = c(1, 2),
      baseline = 1
    ),
    "Columns with NA"
  )
  expect_error(
    shap_conc_decomp(shap = shap, rank = c(1, NA), baseline = 1),
    "missing values"
  )
  expect_error(
    shap_conc_decomp(shap = shap, rank = c(1, 2), baseline = 1,
      weights = c(1, NA)
    ),
    "weights"
  )
  expect_error(
    shap_conc_decomp(shap = shap, rank = c(-1, 1), type = "L", baseline = 1),
    "mean socioeconomic level"
  )
})
```


```{r development-inflate, eval=FALSE}
# Run but keep eval=FALSE to avoid infinite loop
# Execute in the console directly
fusen::inflate(
  flat_file = "dev/shap_decomposition.Rmd",
  vignette_name = "shap_decomposition"
)
```
